methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Alpine Eagle, a seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Jan-Hendrik Boelens and Timo Breuer, has raised €10.25M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a fragmented comp universe: 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems. This 10-company set reflects the broader Defense & Security sector, though precise stage and tier alignment remains constrained.

Standard peer-median methodology was not applicable due to insufficient clustering within Alpine Eagle's specific stage and valuation tier. While 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, only one operates at an adjacent development stage, and none form a cohesive comparable set of three or more firms meeting multiple narrow filters simultaneously. Relying on all-sector medians risked systematic distortion.

The analysis therefore reaches no triangulated valuation range at this stage. Key uncertainties limiting precision include: the heterogeneity of the Defense & Security comp universe across development maturity, the scarcity of seed-stage private market transactions in this sector, and the limited relevance of public comparables to early-stage risk profiles. Further clarity on Alpine Eagle's specific application domain, customer traction, or IP defensibility would be required to refine methodology selection or identify more tightly clustered peer transactions.
